differential_diagnoses: Aortic dissection or penetrating aortic ulcer
next_steps: Urgent transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) to evaluate for valvular vegetations and aortic root involvement
Urgent CT angiography (CTA) of the chest and abdomen to reassess the aorta for progression of mycotic aneurysm or evidence of dissection
Immediate consultation with Infectious Disease, Vascular Surgery, and Cardiothoracic Surgery
Maintain parenteral antistaphylococcal antibiotic therapy (e.g., vancomycin or cefazolin based on sensitivity)
Obtain serial blood cultures to document clearance of bacteremia
Strict blood pressure control (e.g., beta-blockers) to reduce aortic wall stress
Evaluate for surgical versus conservative management of the aortic pathology
